name: Haoyang Li
subtitle: Software Engineer in San Francisco Bay Area
summary: This page is an attempt to re-organize my life into entangled nodes connected by common traits. So we can explore my life not in a rigid categorical way, instead, we can explore it by connections.

blocks.text: I build software across product surfaces, infrastructure, and interface systems. The graph on the home page is a map of the work, places, writing, and projects that most shaped how I think.
I wanted this site to feel closer to exploration than to a linear resume. Each node is a moment with its own texture; the edges try to capture why two moments belong in the same conversation.
The center node is intentionally personal: it is the anchor for the rest of the archive. From there, the graph branches into research, education, travel, writing, experience, and projects.

The style toggle changes the photographic frame of the site without changing the structure beneath it. That felt right for a graph built around memory: the facts stay put, but the way they are remembered can shift.
